generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance VehicleActuatorController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Manages adaptive cruise control actuation signals (speed commands, braking thresholds) transmitted from cloud-based processing to onboard ECUs"
]

instance ServoActuator : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering servo system for ACC vehicle control, validated through feedback integration with virtual commands"
]

instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Drive motor system for ACC speed adjustment, interfaced with cloud-processed desired speed commands"
]

instance ActuationCommands : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ServoActuator, MotorActuator
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Advanced LiDAR system for point cloud generation and object detection in proximity to lead vehicles"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Onboard sensors aggregating real-time vehicle metrics (speed, position, acceleration) for ACC monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateMetrics
]

instance ObjectTrackingSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Point cloud clustering and tracking component for detecting and predicting object trajectories"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ObjectDetectionTracks
]

instance DistanceSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Proximity sensor measuring distance-to-lead vehicle for ACC safety thresholds"
    DTDFVocab:producedData DistanceToLead
]

// C4: Data Transmitted
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance VehicleStateMetrics : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance ObjectDetectionTracks : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ObjectTrackingSensor
]

instance DistanceToLead : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DistanceSensor
]

// C5: Data
instance RawLiDARData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Unprocessed LiDAR sensor readings before point cloud conversion"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:storageFormat "Binary point cloud format"
]

instance ProcessedVehicleState : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated vehicle state metrics (speed, position, acceleration) after sensor fusion"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateMetrics
    DTDFVocab:storageFormat "Structured JSON/CSV"
]

instance ObjectDetectionResults : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Clustering and tracking outputs showing detected objects with predicted trajectories"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ObjectDetectionTracks
    DTDFVocab:storageFormat "Trajectory prediction matrices"
]

instance DistanceMetrics : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time distance measurements to lead vehicles for ACC safety calculations"
    DTDFVocab:fromData DistanceToLead
    DTDFVocab:storageFormat "Numeric arrays (meters)"
]

instance ActuationFeedback : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Feedback from servo/motor actuators validating virtual commands"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ActuationCommands
    DTDFVocab:storageFormat "Binary actuator response logs"
]

instance HistoricalVehicleData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Saved historical vehicle state data for reporting and analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessedVehicleState
    DTDFVocab:storageFormat "Time-series database (SQL/NoSQL)"
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of vehicle state data (speed, projected driving path, object tracks) with UI capabilities for viewing, saving, and generating reports"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVisualizationInsight, HistoricalReportAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Cloud-based ACC processing offloading lead-vehicle detection and speed adjustment algorithms from onboard ECUs to cloud infrastructure"
    DTDFVocab:provides DeterministicWorkflowAction, LatencyOptimizedProcessing
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Operator-focused controls for enabling/disabling ACC, setting desired speeds, and sending emergency braking commands with safety-critical constraints"
    DTDFVocab:provides OperatorControlAction, DeveloperDataCollection
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "3D visualization of projected driving paths, object detection tracks, and state anomalies for real-time monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:provides PathProjectionInsight, AnomalyDetectionInsight
]

// C7: Models
instance AdaptiveCruiseControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model implementing ACC algorithms with deterministic workflow for speed adjustment based on real-time distance-to-lead metrics"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CloudComputeEnabler
]

instance PointCloudProcessingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric model handling LiDAR point cloud conversion, clustering, and tracking for object detection"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2MiddlewareEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model simulating vehicle dynamics and state transitions based on sensor inputs"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
]

instance ProximitySafetyModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Safety-critical model enforcing distance thresholds and braking constraints for ACC operations"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo LatencyOptimizationEnabler
]

// C10: Enablers
instance CloudComputeEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Distributed cloud infrastructure (Kubernetes/Docker) for offloading ACC computation from onboard ECUs"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance ROS2MiddlewareEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Robot Operating System 2 for intra-system communication between sensing components and digital twin entity"
    DTDFVocab:enables PointCloudProcessingModel
]

instance DataPreProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Feature Engineering Entities for preprocessing raw sensor data (noise filtering, coordinate transformation)"
    DTDFVocab:enables VehicleStateSimulationModel
]

instance LatencyOptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time transport protocols (WebSockets/gRPC) ensuring 5ms end-to-end latency for critical ACC metrics"
    DTDFVocab:enables ProximitySafetyModel
]

instance DataStorageEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Centralized database (SQL/NoSQL) for storing vehicle state logs, reports, and troubleshooting data"
    DTDFVocab:enables HistoricalReportAction
]

instance HumanInLoopEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Liberty-to-act vs. Ability-to-act taxonomy framework implementing human-in-the-loop autonomy principles"
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

// C11: Additional Components
instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Hosts device-specific functionalities (Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking) and general automotive resources (sensors, actuators, compute) on onboard hardware"
    base:contains LiDARSensor, VehicleStateSensor, ServoActuator, MotorActuator
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Centralized entity managing data preprocessing, actuation, and control mechanisms for system-wide data orchestration"
    base:contains AdaptiveCruiseControlModel, PointCloudProcessingModel, VehicleStateSimulationModel
]

instance UserInterfaceFeature : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "User-facing components (3D Visualization and Control Panel) as subcomponents of the User Entity for modular interaction design"
    base:contains VisualizationService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataCollectionEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Manages data collection and reporting for troubleshooting during development phases"
    base:contains HistoricalVehicleData, ObjectDetectionResults
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Immediate display of vehicle state data (speed, projected driving path, detected object tracks) in real-time dashboards"
]

instance HistoricalReportAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generation and saving of historical vehicle state reports for analysis and debugging"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DeterministicWorkflowAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Execution of deterministic ACC workflow computing desired speed based on real-time distance-to-lead vehicle metrics"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance LatencyOptimizedProcessing : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Ensuring 5ms end-to-end latency for critical ACC processing through optimized protocols and cloud offloading"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance OperatorControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote enabling/disabling of ACC, setting desired speeds, and sending emergency braking commands"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DeveloperDataCollection : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Data collection and reporting mechanisms for developer troubleshooting during system validation"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance PathProjectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualization of projected driving paths based on real-time sensor data and object detection tracks"
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time detection and visualization of state anomalies (sensor failures, control deviations) in the ACC system"
]

// C18: Environments
instance AutonomousVehicleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains PhysicalTwinEntity, DigitalTwinEntity, UserInterfaceFeature
    base:desc "Teleoperated 1/10th-scale autonomous vehicle operating in highway driving scenarios with ACC functionality"
]

instance DevelopmentTestingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains DataCollectionEntity, CloudComputeEnabler
    base:desc "Specialized environment for data-driven troubleshooting and system validation during development phases"
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
    **Not in Document**
  "
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system enforces **time synchronization** between physical and digital components with a **100 ms accuracy requirement**, critical for safety-critical vehicular applications. This precision is mandated by hardware limitations and ensures real-time coordination for computation offloading tasks. The system does not specify explicit synchronization protocols (e.g., NTP, PTP) but implies the need for high-precision clock synchronization frameworks to meet the 100 ms latency threshold, aligning with stricter vehicular standards beyond the baseline 100 ms tolerance."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system’s **orchestration** is structured around the ISO 23247 Reference Architecture (RA), adapted via three primary entities (Physical Twin, Digital Twin, User) mapped to ROS2 packages. Key integration patterns include:
- **Component Mapping**: Each ISO 23247 Feature (FE) is either implemented or omitted (e.g., the Data Storage FE lacks a natural FE alignment). The User Interface FE is split into 3D Visualization and Control Panel components.
- **Hardware Deployment**: Entities are deployed on distinct hardware, enabling parallel execution of functionalities (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering on sensors/actuators). This deviates from ISO 23247’s OME model by embedding broader automotive capabilities into the Physical Twin Entity.
- **System Coordination**: The system relies on ROS2’s middleware for inter-entity communication, facilitating real-time data exchange between software components. The absence of explicit orchestration protocols (e.g., KubeOPS, Zephyr) suggests ROS2’s built-in message-passing (e.g., ROS2 topics/publishers) handles coordination, though the 100 ms synchronization constraint may impose latency-sensitive constraints on message frequency.

Resource management is implicit in the entity-based deployment, where hardware ownership dictates functional responsibility. No mention of federated twins or cross-twin coordination mechanisms is provided."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The ISO 23247 standard outlines a **reference architecture (RA)** for Digital Twins in manufacturing, but does **not explicitly define lifecycle stages** as a structured sequence. However, the framework implicitly supports lifecycle phases through its **entity-based architecture**, where each **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)** and its associated entities (e.g., Data Collection and Device Control Entity, Digital Twin Entity, User Entity) evolve alongside the physical system they represent.

Key **representation types** at different stages are implied by the **entity roles**:
- **Development Phase**: Functional Entities (FEs) are designed and implemented (e.g., using OPC UA for data exchange) to model the OME’s behavior, data flows, and control interfaces.
- **Operational Phase**: The Digital Twin Entity dynamically synchronizes with the OME via real-time data collection (e.g., sensors, IoT devices) and device control (e.g., actuators) to mirror its physical state.
- **Transition Management**: The standard does not specify formalized transition protocols, but the **entity-based RA** ensures seamless handoff between development (e.g., simulation/testing) and operational phases by maintaining consistent data models and interfaces (e.g., OPC UA for interoperability).

Lifecycle governance is **implicitly governed by the ISO 23247’s Information Exchange Part**, which mandates compliance with standards like OPC UA for data consistency and interoperability, ensuring the Digital Twin’s accuracy and reliability across lifecycle stages.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system’s fidelity and validity are governed by the **ISO 23247** reference architecture, where the **Digital Twin Entity (DTE)** must accurately reflect the **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**. Validation and verification are implicitly supported through the **entity-based decomposition**, ensuring consistency between physical and digital representations. However, the standard does not specify explicit methods for uncertainty quantification, error detection, or quality assurance processes beyond the overarching framework. No direct mention of model fidelity thresholds or validation protocols is provided.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
    **Network Infrastructure:**
    The DTS employs a **hybrid architecture** with:
    - **Intra-Entity Communication:** ROS2-based **ROS2 network** (ROS2 is a middleware for real-time robotics, enabling low-latency message passing between software components).
    - **Inter-Entity Communication:** **Wi-Fi UDP** (UDP ensures low-latency, high-throughput data transfer for critical sensor streams, though UDP lacks reliability guarantees; redundancy may be implied for real-time systems).

    **Topology & Architecture:**
    The system follows a **modular microservices architecture** (as depicted in Fig. 2), where:
    - **Physical Twin Entity** (sensors/actuators) connects to **Data Translation FE** (serialization/unit conversion).
    - **Software Components** (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering, Actuation FE) communicate via ROS2 topics.
    - **Edge/Cloud Deployment** is implied for processing (e.g., LiDAR data clustering may run on edge devices, while reporting FE could reside in a cloud-based database).

    **Bandwidth & Latency Requirements:**
    - **LiDAR Point Clouds:** High-bandwidth requirements (~100–1000 points/sec) for real-time clustering/tracking; latency must be <100ms for accurate object detection.
    - **Vehicle State Updates:** Frequent updates (e.g., 100Hz for motor/servo commands) require low-latency communication.
    - **Actuation FE:** Real-time control loops (e.g., servo positioning) demand <50ms response times.

    **Reliability & Redundancy:**
    - **ROS2’s Built-in Reliability:** ROS2 supports message retries and deadlock detection (though UDP lacks acknowledgments).
    - **Wi-Fi Redundancy:** Implied for critical paths (e.g., dual Wi-Fi adapters or failover mechanisms for sensor data).
    - **Data Translation FE:** Acts as a single point of failure mitigation via message validation (e.g., unit consistency checks).

    **Security Protocols:**
    - **Data Translation FE:** Ensures type safety and unit consistency (prevents malformed commands).
    - **ROS2 Security:** Implicitly supports authentication (e.g., ROS2 security layers for message validation).
    - **Wi-Fi Encryption:** UDP traffic is not encrypted by default; assumed to use **WPA2/WPA3** for physical network security (not explicitly stated in documents).
  "
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
    **Deployment Infrastructure:**
    The DTS is deployed across **edge and cloud hybrid architectures**, with:
    - **Edge Deployment:**
      - **On-vehicle Processing:** Components like **Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking**, **Data Pre-Processing FE**, and **Actuation FE** run on **ROS2-enabled edge devices** (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson, Intel NUC) for low-latency processing.
      - **Computational Requirements:** Real-time processing demands **multi-core CPUs (e.g., 4+ cores)** and **high-speed GPUs** (e.g., NVIDIA RTX for LiDAR processing).
      - **Storage:** Temporary storage for raw point clouds (e.g., 1GB–10GB for 10–60 seconds of data at 100Hz).

    - **Cloud Deployment:**
      - **Reporting FE & Data Storage:** Hosted in a **cloud database** (e.g., AWS S3, PostgreSQL) for long-term storage and reporting.
      - **Computational Requirements:** Scalable servers for batch processing (e.g., LiDAR data archiving, historical analysis).
      - **Scalability Mechanisms:**
        - **ROS2’s Horizontal Scaling:** Supports distributed nodes for parallel processing (e.g., clustering across multiple edge devices).
        - **Wi-Fi Load Balancing:** Implied for high-availability sensor data ingestion (e.g., load-balanced UDP streams).

    **Deployment Models Supported:**
    - **On-Premise Edge:** Direct integration with vehicle systems (e.g., OEM deployment).
    - **Cloud-Edge Hybrid:** Edge processing for real-time control, cloud for analytics/reporting.
    - **Software-Defined Architecture (SDA):** ROS2’s modularity enables dynamic component deployment (e.g., swapping FE components for different vehicle models).

    **Availability & Reliability Assurance:**
    - **99.9% Uptime:** Implied for critical paths (e.g., motor/servo actuation FE must be highly available).
    - **Redundancy Strategies:**
      - **Sensor Redundancy:** Multiple LiDAR/vehicle state sensors for cross-validation.
      - **Component Redundancy:** ROS2’s node failover mechanisms (e.g., if a clustering node crashes, another takes over).
    - **Fault Tolerance:** Data Translation FE validates all messages before processing, preventing silent failures.
  "
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin system** must incorporate **data governance frameworks** aligned with **ISO 26262-1:2018** for functional safety in road vehicles, ensuring compliance with privacy regulations implicitly through adherence to safety-critical standards. While explicit data ownership policies are not detailed in the provided documents, the system should enforce **consent management** for data sharing in **v2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services** as per **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0**, where user consent is a prerequisite for enabling communication between vehicles and external systems (e.g., roadside units, cloud platforms).

For **privacy protection**, the system should implement **data anonymization** techniques compliant with **GDPR-like principles**, though no specific methodologies are outlined. The **data sharing agreements** would likely involve **encryption standards** (e.g., AES-256) for securing transmitted data in v2X communications, as implied by the technical specification’s focus on secure service requirements. No explicit mention of **data residency laws** or **third-party access controls** is present, but the system’s architecture should prioritize **end-to-end encryption** for sensitive vehicle data to mitigate unauthorized access risks.

No explicit mention of **data lifecycle management** (e.g., retention policies, deletion protocols) is provided, but the **ISO 15622** standard for adaptive cruise control systems suggests a focus on **real-time data integrity**, implying transient storage and validation mechanisms for critical vehicle telemetry.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The ISO 23247 standard is the **core compliance framework** for this Digital Twin system, with the following **specifications and interoperability standards**:
1. **Industry Standards**:
   - **ISO 23247-2 (Reference Architecture)**: Defines the entity-based RA (e.g., OME, Data Collection Entity, Digital Twin Entity).
   - **ISO 23247-4 (Information Exchange)**: Mandates compliance with **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** for data exchange, ensuring interoperability between entities (e.g., sensors, PLCs, user interfaces).
2. **Certification Requirements**:
   - The standard does not specify **formal certification bodies**, but adherence to OPC UA and ISO 23247’s principles (e.g., data integrity) would imply **compliance validation** via third-party audits (e.g., ISO 17025).
3. **Compliance Frameworks**:
   - The system aligns with **ISO 14224 (Digital Twin terminology)** for terminology consistency and **ISO 37104 (Digital Twin framework)** principles (though not explicitly cited).
4. **Interoperability Standards**:
   - **OPC UA** is the **primary protocol** for data/control interfaces, enabling seamless integration with legacy systems (e.g., SCADA, ERP).
   - No other interoperability standards (e.g., MQTT, ROS) are mentioned, but the RA’s modularity supports extensibility.

**Not in Document**: No details on cloud-native deployment, cybersecurity standards (e.g., ISO/IEC 27001), or specific certification bodies.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin system** must adhere to **ISO 26262-1:2018** for **functional safety**, ensuring compliance with **ASIL (Automotive Safety Integrity Level) requirements** for safety-critical components (e.g., adaptive cruise control systems as defined in **ISO 15622:2018**). This includes **fail-safe mechanisms** such as redundant sensors, fault detection, and isolation (FDI) systems to prevent catastrophic failures.

For **cybersecurity**, the system must implement **v2X service protocols** per **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0**, which mandates **authentication and authorization** via **X.509 certificates** and **IPsec** for securing vehicle-to-infrastructure (V2I) and vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V) communications. **Threat mitigation strategies** would include **real-time intrusion detection** (e.g., using anomaly-based algorithms) and **zero-trust architecture principles**, though no specific tools or frameworks are detailed.

The system should conduct **risk assessments** using **ISO 26262’s hazard analysis methods** (e.g., FTA/FMEA) to identify and mitigate cyber-physical risks. **Access control mechanisms** would enforce **role-based access** (RBAC) for Twin operators, with **multi-factor authentication (MFA)** for high-risk operations. **Fail-safe mechanisms** for the Digital Twin cockpit (as referenced in **Dalibor et al., 2020**) would include **redundant computing nodes** and **automatic fallback to backup systems** during hardware failures, ensuring continuous operation of critical functions (e.g., collision avoidance).

No explicit mention of **quantum-resistant cryptography** or **post-quantum algorithms** is provided, but the system’s architecture should prioritize **future-proofing** against evolving cyber threats, particularly in **v2X ecosystems** where latency-sensitive data exchange is paramount.
"
]
